toolResult.reason: Wrote design.md, spec.md, and tasks.md under specrunner/changes/bite-test-executor/ on branch change/bite-test-executor-a58cc81e. The plan covers: (D1) resolving isolated-worktree dependencies by symlinking the job worktree's node_modules (fail-closed if absent); (D2) an opt-in provider-neutral VerificationConfig.scopedTestCommand string + validation; (D3) per-file scoped execution under custom commands reusing the verification spawnCommand PATH/.bin executor, with the default and managed paths unchanged; (D4) never-throw + finally cleanup of worktree and symlink; (D5) no port-signature change (rides on existing config param); (D6) real-LocalRuntime integration + end-to-end tooth proofs. Tasks T-01..T-06 map to acceptance criteria T1-T6, and explicitly forbid editing .specrunner/config.json and src/core/port/runtime-strategy.ts.

observations.title: T5 gate test needs forward-type request state (FORWARD_TYPES constraint)
observations.rationale: runBiteEvidenceGate returns strategy-deferred immediately for non-forward types. T5 must use request.type = 'bug-fix' or 'new-feature' in the job state. tasks.md says 'follow existing gate/floor test state shape' without naming this constraint explicitly. An implementer reading gate.ts would find it, but an explicit note reduces risk.

observations.title: scopedTestCommand trust boundary not documented
observations.rationale: scopedTestCommand is passed as-is to sh -c, same as verification.commands. The project-author trust boundary is the same, so no new attack surface is introduced, but design.md does not explicitly state this equivalence. testFile shell-quoting is documented; scopedTestCommand's trust model is not.

observations.title: No acceptance criterion for testFile paths with special characters
observations.rationale: The single-quote escaping algorithm for testFile is specified in T-02 but T4's acceptance criteria do not include a case with spaces or single quotes in the file path to verify the escaping works end-to-end. Low risk in practice (git-managed paths rarely contain special chars), but the gap exists.

message: spec-review iteration 1 completed with verdict: approved
